files_changed.summary: Pure-data MATERIALS map with 3 seed entries (mesquite_wood, structural_steel, copper). Exports MATERIALS, MATERIAL_IDS, getMaterial(id). Includes JSDoc references and per-entry property comments.

Pure stress-strain simulation. Exports runTensileTest, runCompressionTest, generateStressStrainCurve, calculateForce, calculateDisplacement, detectFailurePoint, _smokeTest. Models elastic Hooke's-law region, exponential plastic hardening for ductile metals, brittle plateau-then-drop for wood, anisotropic grain-direction multipliers, compression knockdowns per family.

Rule-of-mixtures composite synthesis. Exports createComposite, estimateCompositeProperties, COMPOSITE_PRESETS. Handles laminated wood as a special case via grainDirectionMultiplier.laminated. Two TBD presets (carbon_fiber_polymer, glass_fiber_polymer) returned as pending stubs with todo strings.

Use-case-aware scoring. Exports scoreMaterialForBridge and BRIDGE_COLLAPSE_USE_CASE. Priority-driven weighted sum across stw/strength/cost/corrosion/fatigue, with environment-tag corrosion multipliers, additive bridgeScoreBias correction, threshold gate at strengthScore < 0.30 for non-cost priorities, and warning rules for monsoon/cross-grain/fatigue.

materialDatabase.js: USDA Forest Products Laboratory Wood Handbook (2010, FPL-GTR-190) — wood mechanical properties parallel- and cross-grain, density, Young's modulus, ultimate tensile strength
Engineering Toolbox — Young's modulus and density for common copper and steel alloys
ASTM A36 / A572 spec sheets — yield and ultimate ranges for structural steel
materialTestingEngine.js: USDA Forest Products Laboratory Wood Handbook (2010, FPL-GTR-190) — anisotropic wood behavior, cross-grain knockdown factors
Engineering Toolbox — Young's modulus / Poisson ratio / typical ductile metal stress-strain shapes
ASTM E8 / E9 — tensile and compression test methodology informs the curve shape
compositeEngine.js: Krenchel, H. (1964) 'Fibre Reinforcement' — orientation efficiency factors for unidirectional, woven, and random fiber arrangements
Engineering Toolbox / standard composites texts — rule-of-mixtures formulae
USDA Forest Products Lab Wood Handbook — laminated wood beam stiffness multipliers
bridgeMaterialScoring.js: AASHTO LRFD Bridge Design Specifications — high-level guidance on selecting structural materials by load class and environment
USDA Forest Products Lab Wood Handbook — cross-grain knockdowns informing 'warning' rules
Engineering Toolbox — corrosion behavior of mild steel vs copper informing environment-tag weights

smoke_test_summary.method: Ran `node --input-type=module -e "import('./materialTestingEngine.js').then(m => console.log(JSON.stringify(m._smokeTest())))"`. All three materials returned non-null TestResults for both tensile and compression.

compression.comment: 60% of tensile ultimate per spec — wood is weaker in compression
symmetric to tension; buckling not modeled per spec
1.5× tensile fracture strain budget — copper crushes/squishes rather than breaks

mesquite_wood.recommendation: Not suitable as primary structure — consider for decorative or sensor roles
mesquite_wood.trigger: strengthScore 0.22 < 0.30 threshold for 'balanced' priority — gate fired correctly

structural_steel.recommendation: Excellent — primary structural element for this use case
warnings: Corrosion resistance is low — monsoon environment will accelerate degradation

copper.recommendation: Good — viable as a main beam, double-check fatigue under cyclic load

composite_check.laminated_mesquite: E=13.75 GPa (1.25× mesquite parallel), ultimate=125 MPa — matches grainDirectionMultiplier.laminated of 1.25
composite_check.copper_wire_reinforced_wood: E=17.7 GPa, ultimate=106.25 MPa, density=1661 kg/m³ — Voigt blend with woven η=0.65, 0.10 Vf copper
composite_check.carbon_fiber_polymer: pending=true with todo string — no crash, scene can render as locked
composite_check.steel_cable_reinforced_composite: E=16.95 GPa, density=1200 kg/m³ — Voigt blend with woven η=0.65, 0.05 Vf steel

gameplay_simplifications_flagged: Wood compression strength = 60% of tensile ultimate. Real wood compression depends on grain direction (perpendicular crushing is much weaker than parallel); we collapse this to one number for gameplay clarity. Documented at the top of materialTestingEngine.js.

Copper plastic hardening uses sigma = sigma_u * (1 - exp(-8 * plastic_strain)). The hardening exponent of 8 is phenomenological — chosen to make the curve approach ultimate by ~50% of the plastic-strain budget. Real copper follows Hollomon (sigma = K * eps^n with n≈0.45 for annealed) which would be more accurate but harder to tune visually. Documented in materialTestingEngine.js header.
No strain-rate effects. Real metals stiffen at high rates; we model quasi-static behavior. The strainRateHz option only controls chart sample density. Documented.
Wood proportional limit set at 80% of ultimate (since yieldStrengthMPa is null). Real value varies 60–80% of ultimate depending on species; 80% chosen as a clean default.

Composite strength uses the same rule-of-mixtures form as stiffness. Real composite strength is governed by the weaker phase failing first, but the simplified form keeps gameplay numbers in a believable range. Documented in compositeEngine.js header.
Composite cost adds a 10% surcharge on the fiber's cost component to model manufacturing premium. Hand-tuned, not derived.
Toughness/corrosion/fatigue/bridgeScoreBias are 0–1 gameplay scalars, not unit-bearing values. Documented at top of materialDatabase.js.
Bridge score's environment effect only re-weights corrosionResistance. UV / freeze-thaw / fatigue environment interactions are not modeled. Documented in bridgeMaterialScoring.js header.
Bridge bias is added at 0.10× the raw bias (so a +1 bias only adds 0.10 to the final score). Prevents bias from dominating priority weighting.

scope_compliance.files_touched: Only the four new files under src/renderer/game/systems/materials/. Verified via `git status -s` — only the new directory and the orchestrator-managed .claude/swarm/state.json are modified.

notes.node_syntax_check: All four files passed `node --check`. No parse errors.
follow_ups_for_wave_2: utm-material-lab-scene (Wave 2) — consume runTensileTest/runCompressionTest from a Phaser scene; render the curve with one of the existing chart helpers; surface unlockedKnowledge into the questSystem.
Once a polymer matrix material is added in a future wave, populate carbon_fiber_polymer and glass_fiber_polymer presets by re-running createComposite with real ids.
The bridge_collapse quest's currently-fake material testing should be replaced with a flow that calls runTensileTest + scoreMaterialForBridge(result, BRIDGE_COLLAPSE_USE_CASE).
